strategies.name: Risk Analysis & Information Security Policies
strategies.description: Policies on the analysis of risks and on the security of information systems, including risk assessments, information classification, and governance controls.

ML1.summary: Establish foundational governance controls: enable audit logging, restrict third-party application consent, and enforce admin consent workflows.

ML2.summary: Deploy sensitivity labels across all workloads, configure auto-labeling, implement DLP policies for regulated data types, and enable access reviews.

ML3.summary: Enable insider risk management, adaptive protection, endpoint DLP, information barriers, and Power BI DLP for comprehensive risk coverage.

strategies.name: Incident Handling
strategies.description: Incident detection, classification, response, and recovery procedures to meet NIS2 reporting obligations (24h early warning, 72h notification, 1-month final report).

ML1.summary: Onboard all device types to Defender for Endpoint, enable tamper protection, configure alert notifications, and retain audit logs for at least one year.

ML2.summary: Enable EDR in block mode, automated investigation and remediation, deception technology, threat intelligence indicators, and DLP incident detection.

ML3.summary: Deploy communication compliance policies for detecting regulatory violations and offensive language to identify insider threats.

strategies.name: Business Continuity & Crisis Management
strategies.description: Business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery, and crisis management including data retention and recovery capabilities.

ML1.summary: Configure retention policies across all workloads with long-term retention, retain audit logs, and block infected file downloads to protect data integrity.

ML2.summary: Deploy classification-based retention labels and records management with disposition review for structured data lifecycle management.

ML3.summary: Enable regulatory hold capabilities for financial services and critical sector compliance.

strategies.name: Supply Chain Security
strategies.description: Security measures for relationships between entities and their direct suppliers or service providers, including third-party access controls and data sharing governance.

ML1.summary: Restrict third-party app consent, control guest invitations, restrict external sharing to existing guests, enable B2B integration, block external mail forwarding, and restrict Teams federation.

ML2.summary: Restrict external sharing to allowed domains, enforce anonymous link expiry, require MFA for guests, block external file sharing in Teams to unapproved storage, and control guest resharing.

ML3.summary: Disable calendar sharing externally, block Teams channel email addresses, and enforce restricted access control on sensitive sites.

strategies.name: Secure Acquisition, Development & Vulnerability Handling
strategies.description: Security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling, disclosure, and patch management.

ML1.summary: Configure automated patching via Windows Update rings, enable vulnerability management scanning, enforce modern authentication, and disable custom scripts on SharePoint.

ML2.summary: Deploy exploit protection system-wide mitigations, network protection in block mode, and CIS-aligned security baselines for Windows and Edge.

ML3.summary: Enable all 16 ASR rules in block mode, Credential Guard and Device Guard, and controlled folder access for ransomware protection.

strategies.name: Effectiveness Assessment
strategies.description: Policies and procedures to assess the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures through testing, monitoring, and auditing.

ML1.summary: Enable unified audit logging with comprehensive SharePoint audit events and minimum one-year log retention as the foundation for effectiveness measurement.

ML2.summary: Conduct periodic access reviews for privileged roles, continuous vulnerability assessment, and insider risk behavioural analysis.

ML3.summary: Deploy deception technology for proactive security testing and adaptive protection that dynamically adjusts DLP policies based on risk signals.

strategies.name: Cyber Hygiene Practices & Training
strategies.description: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training, including password management, endpoint protection, email security, and security awareness.

ML1.summary: Enforce custom banned password lists, enable self-service password reset, block legacy and basic authentication, deploy anti-phishing policies, and configure Defender Antivirus.

ML2.summary: Deploy the full Defender for Office 365 suite (Safe Links, Safe Attachments, anti-malware, auto-purge, common attachment filter), enable user reporting, and mobile app protection policies.

ML3.summary: Enable web content filtering, disable LinkedIn connections, hide KMSI prompt, and limit Windows diagnostic data collection.

strategies.name: Cryptography & Encryption
strategies.description: Policies and procedures regarding the use of cryptography and, where appropriate, encryption to protect data at rest and in transit.

ML1.summary: Enforce BitLocker drive encryption, configure DKIM and DMARC for email authentication, and require modern authentication for all services.

ML2.summary: Deploy sensitivity labels with encryption across workloads, configure Windows Hello for Business for cryptographic authentication, and enforce OAuth 2.0.

ML3.summary: Enable Credential Guard for credential protection, and DLP policies to block source code, credentials, and password sharing externally.

strategies.name: Human Resources Security, Access Control & Asset Management
strategies.description: Human resources security including access control policies, privileged access management, device management, and asset lifecycle controls.

ML1.summary: Require MFA for all users, block legacy authentication, restrict guest invitations and device registration, enable access reviews, and restrict device enrollment to corporate devices.

ML2.summary: Block high-risk and medium-risk sign-ins, enforce geo-blocking, sign-in frequency, compliant devices, and deploy device compliance baselines across all platforms.

ML3.summary: Block unsupported platforms, enable LAPS, enforce firewall policies, control removable storage, restrict SharePoint by IP range, and configure idle session timeouts.

strategies.name: Multi-Factor Authentication & Secured Communications
strategies.description: Use of multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication solutions, and secured voice, video and text communications including secured emergency communications.

ML1.summary: Require MFA for all users, block legacy authentication, configure Authenticator with number matching and additional context, and require MFA for Azure management.

ML2.summary: Require phishing-resistant MFA for admins, MFA for Azure Service Management API, managed device for security info registration, MFA for device enrollment, and compliant devices.

ML3.summary: Enforce sign-in frequency for all users and every-time for admins, deploy Windows Hello for Business, and secure Teams communications (lobby, anonymous join, presenters, recording).
